rationale: The page contains detailed scientific information about CERN, the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), matter-antimatter asymmetry, and the distribution of ordinary matter in the universe. This content is highly suitable for generating factual and conceptual questions.
cleanedText:
More on the News
* Breakthrough Prize 2025 is awarded to researchers from more than 70 countries representing four experimental collaborations at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider (LHC).
* It has been awarded for discovery of new strongly interacting particles, study of rare processes and matter-antimatter asymmetry, etc.
About CERN (European Organization for Nuclear Research)
* An international scientific organization established for the purpose of collaborative research into high-energy particle physics.
* Location: Franco-Swiss border
* India is an Associate Member.
* Major Achievements
* LHC confirmed the existence of the Higgs boson (Also, known as God particle).
* Higgs Boson is a subatomic particle that was first theorized by physicist Peter Higgs and others.
* Development of the World Wide Web
About CERN’s LHC
* World’s largest and most powerful particle accelerator.
* Consists of superconducting magnets with a number of accelerating structures to boost the energy of the particles along the way.
* Inside the accelerator, two high-energy particle beams travel at close to the speed of light before they are made to collide.
Four experimental collaborations at CERN’s LHC
* A Toroidal LHC Apparatus (ATLAS)
* Compact Muon Solenoid (CMS)
* A Large Ion Collider Experiment (ALICE)
* Large Hadron Collider beauty (LHCb)
Matter–Antimatter Asymmetry
* A major milestone in understanding the matter-antimatter asymmetry in the universe as CERN’s scientists has observed CP violation in baryons for the first time.
* CP symmetry implies laws of physics should remain unchanged if particles are swapped with their antiparticles and viewed in a mirror.
* About Matter–Antimatter Asymmetry
* Refers to the observed imbalance between matter and antimatter in the universe.
* According to modern physics, the Big Bang should have produced equal amounts of matter and antimatter, yet the observable universe consists almost entirely of matter, with antimatter nearly absent.
* If they come in contact, they annihilate one another, leaving behind pure energy.
3.4. ORDINARY MATTER
Why in the News?
Harvard & Smithsonian (CfA) and Caltech, for the first time, detected the universe’s missing ordinary matter using powerful cosmic signals known as Fast Radio Bursts (FRBs).
Distribution of Ordinary Matter Across the Universe
* 76% in intergalactic medium (IGM) (the thin gas between galaxies).
* 15% in galaxy halos (outer regions surrounding galaxies)
* 9% within galaxies (consist of stars, planets, and vast clouds of gas and dust, all bound together by gravity).
